NKJV.1: Then the whole multitude of them arose and led Him to Pilate.
NKJV.2: And they began to accuse Him, saying, “We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to pay taxes to Caesar, saying that He Himself is Christ, a King.”
NKJV.3: Then Pilate asked Him, saying, “Are You the King of the Jews?” He answered him and said, “It is as you say.”
NKJV.4: So Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowd, “I find no fault in this Man.”
NKJV.5: But they were the more fierce, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee to this place.”
NKJV.6: When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked if the Man were a Galilean.
NKJV.7: And as soon as he knew that He belonged to Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
NKJV.8: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad; for he had desired for a long time to see Him, because he had heard many things about Him, and he hoped to see some miracle done by Him.
NKJV.9: Then he questioned Him with many words, but He answered him nothing.
NKJV.10: And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused Him.
NKJV.11: Then Herod, with his men of war, treated Him with contempt and mocked Him, arrayed Him in a gorgeous robe, and sent Him back to Pilate.
NKJV.12: That very day Pilate and Herod became friends with each other, for previously they had been at enmity with each other.
NKJV.13: Then Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,
NKJV.14: said to them, “You have brought this Man to me, as one who misleads the people. And indeed, having examined Him in your presence, I have found no fault in this Man concerning those things of which you accuse Him;
NKJV.15: no, neither did Herod, for I sent you back to him; and indeed nothing deserving of death has been done by Him.
NKJV.16: I will therefore chastise Him and release Him”
NKJV.17: (for it was necessary for him to release one to them at the feast).
NKJV.18: And they all cried out at once, saying, “Away with this Man, and release to us Barabbas”—
NKJV.19: who had been thrown into prison for a certain rebellion made in the city, and for murder.
NKJV.20: Pilate, therefore, wishing to release Jesus, again called out to them.
NKJV.21: But they shouted, saying, “Crucify Him, crucify Him!”
NKJV.22: Then he said to them the third time, “Why, what evil has He done? I have found no reason for death in Him. I will therefore chastise Him and let Him go.”
NKJV.23: But they were insistent, demanding with loud voices that He be crucified. And the voices of these men and of the chief priests prevailed.
NKJV.24: So Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they requested.
NKJV.25: And he released to them the one they requested, who for rebellion and murder had been thrown into prison; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
NKJV.26: Now as they led Him away, they laid hold of a certain man, Simon a Cyrenian, who was coming from the country, and on him they laid the cross that he might bear it after Jesus.
NKJV.27: And a great multitude of the people followed Him, and women who also mourned and lamented Him.
NKJV.28: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
NKJV.29: For indeed the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, wombs that never bore, and breasts which never nursed!’
NKJV.30: Then they will begin  ‘to say to the mountains, “Fall on us!” and to the hills, “Cover us!” ’
NKJV.31: For if they do these things in the green wood, what will be done in the dry?”
NKJV.32: There were also two others, criminals, led with Him to be put to death.
NKJV.33: And when they had come to the place called Calvary, there they crucified Him, and the criminals, one on the right hand and the other on the left.
NKJV.34: Then Jesus said, “Father,  forgive them, for  they do not know what they do.” And they divided His garments and cast lots.
NKJV.35: And the people stood looking on. But even the rulers with them sneered, saying, “He saved others; let Him save Himself if He is the Christ, the chosen of God.”
NKJV.36: The soldiers also mocked Him, coming and offering Him sour wine,
NKJV.37: and saying, “If You are the King of the Jews, save Yourself.”
NKJV.38: And an inscription also was written over Him in letters of Greek, Latin, and Hebrew: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
NKJV.39: Then one of the criminals who were hanged blasphemed Him, saying, “If You are the Christ, save Yourself and us.”
NKJV.40: But the other, answering, rebuked him, saying, “Do you not even fear God, seeing you are under the same condemnation?
NKJV.41: And we indeed justly, for we receive the due reward of our deeds; but this Man has done nothing wrong.”
NKJV.42: Then he said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when You come into Your kingdom.”
NKJV.43: And Jesus said to him, “Assuredly, I say to you, today you will be with Me in  Paradise.”
NKJV.44: Now it was about the sixth hour, and there was darkness over all the earth until the ninth hour.
NKJV.45: Then the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
NKJV.46: And when Jesus had cried out with a loud voice, He said, “Father,  ‘into Your hands I commit My spirit.’ ” Having said this, He breathed His last.
NKJV.47: So when the centurion saw what had happened, he glorified God, saying, “Certainly this was a righteous Man!”
NKJV.48: And the whole crowd who came together to that sight, seeing what had been done, beat their breasts and returned.
NKJV.49: But all His acquaintances, and the women who followed Him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
NKJV.50: Now behold, there was a man named Joseph, a council member, a good and just man.
NKJV.51: He had not consented to their decision and deed. He was from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who himself was also waiting for the kingdom of God.
NKJV.52: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
NKJV.53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a tomb that was hewn out of the rock, where no one had ever lain before.
NKJV.54: That day was the Preparation, and the Sabbath drew near.
NKJV.55: And the women who had come with Him from Galilee followed after, and they observed the tomb and how His body was laid.
NKJV.56: Then they returned and prepared spices and fragrant oils. And they rested on the Sabbath according to the commandment.

ESV.1: Then the whole company of them arose and brought him before Pilate.
ESV.2: And they began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man misleading our nation and forbidding us to give tribute to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”
ESV.3: And Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” And he answered him,  “You have said so.”
ESV.4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no guilt in this man.”
ESV.5: But they were urgent, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee even to this place.”
ESV.6: When Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
ESV.7: And when he learned that he belonged to Herod's jurisdiction, he sent him over to Herod, who was himself in Jerusalem at that time.
ESV.8: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had long desired to see him, because he had heard about him, and he was hoping to see some sign done by him.
ESV.9: So he questioned him at some length, but he made no answer.
ESV.10: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.
ESV.11: And Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him. Then, arraying him in splendid clothing, he sent him back to Pilate.
ESV.12: And Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before this they had been at enmity with each other.
ESV.13: Pilate then called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
ESV.14: and said to them, “You brought me this man as one who was misleading the people. And after examining him before you, behold, I did not find this man guilty of any of your charges against him.
ESV.15: Neither did Herod, for he sent him back to us. Look, nothing deserving death has been done by him.
ESV.16: I will therefore punish and release him.”
ESV.18: But they all cried out together, “Away with this man, and release to us Barabbas” —
ESV.19: a man who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection started in the city and for murder.
ESV.20: Pilate addressed them once more, desiring to release Jesus,
ESV.21: but they kept shouting, “Crucify, crucify him!”
ESV.22: A third time he said to them, “Why? What evil has he done? I have found in him no guilt deserving death. I will therefore punish and release him.”
ESV.23: But they were urgent, demanding with loud cries that he should be crucified. And their voices prevailed.
ESV.24: So Pilate decided that their demand should be granted.
ESV.25: He released the man who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus over to their will.
ESV.26: And as they led him away, they seized one Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it behind Jesus.
ESV.27: And there followed him a great multitude of the people and of women who were mourning and lamenting for him.
ESV.28: But turning to them Jesus said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
ESV.29: For behold, the days are coming when they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren and the wombs that never bore and the breasts that never nursed!’
ESV.30: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, ‘Fall on us,’ and to the hills, ‘Cover us.’
ESV.31: For if they do these things when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?”
ESV.32: Two others, who were criminals, were led away to be put to death with him.
ESV.33: And when they came to the place that is called The Skull, there they crucified him, and the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.
ESV.34: And Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.”  And they cast lots to divide his garments.
ESV.35: And the people stood by, watching, but the rulers scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others; let him save himself, if he is the Christ of God, his Chosen One!”
ESV.36: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine
ESV.37: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
ESV.38: There was also an inscription over him,  “This is the King of the Jews.”
ESV.39: One of the criminals who were hanged railed at him,  saying, “Are you not the Christ? Save yourself and us!”
ESV.40: But the other rebuked him, saying, “Do you not fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
ESV.41: And we indeed justly, for we are receiving the due reward of our deeds; but this man has done nothing wrong.”
ESV.42: And he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.”
ESV.43: And he said to him, “Truly, I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.”
ESV.44: It was now about the sixth hour,  and there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour,
ESV.45: while the sun's light failed. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
ESV.46: Then Jesus, calling out with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” And having said this he breathed his last.
ESV.47: Now when the centurion saw what had taken place, he praised God, saying, “Certainly this man was innocent!”
ESV.48: And all the crowds that had assembled for this spectacle, when they saw what had taken place, returned home beating their breasts.
ESV.49: And all his acquaintances and the women who had followed him from Galilee stood at a distance watching these things.
ESV.50: Now there was a man named Joseph, from the Jewish town of Arimathea. He was a member of the council, a good and righteous man,
ESV.51: who had not consented to their decision and action; and he was looking for the kingdom of God.
ESV.52: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
ESV.53: Then he took it down and wrapped it in a linen shroud and laid him in a tomb cut in stone, where no one had ever yet been laid.
ESV.54: It was the day of Preparation, and the Sabbath was beginning.
ESV.55: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed and saw the tomb and how his body was laid.
ESV.56: Then they returned and prepared spices and ointments. On the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

NASB.1: Then the whole body of them got up and brought Him before Pilate.

NASB.3: So Pilate asked Him, saying, 'Are You the King of the Jews?' And He answered him and said, '[It is as] you say.'
NASB.4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, 'I find no guilt in this man.'
NASB.5: But they kept on insisting, saying, 'He stirs up the people, teaching all over Judea, starting from Galilee even as far as this place.'
NASB.6: When Pilate heard it, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
NASB.7: And when he learned that He belonged to Herod's jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who himself also was in Jerusalem at that time.
NASB.8: Now Herod was very glad when he saw Jesus; for he had wanted to see Him for a long time, because he had been hearing about Him and was hoping to see some sign performed by Him.
NASB.9: And he questioned Him at some length; but He answered him nothing.
NASB.10: And the chief priests and the scribes were standing there, accusing Him vehemently.
NASB.11: And Herod with his soldiers, after treating Him with contempt and mocking Him, dressed Him in a gorgeous robe and sent Him back to Pilate.
NASB.12: Now Herod and Pilate became friends with one another that very day; for before they had been enemies with each other.
NASB.13: Pilate summoned the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
NASB.14: and said to them, 'You brought this man to me as one who incites the people to rebellion, and behold, having examined Him before you, I have found no guilt in this man regarding the charges which you make against Him.
NASB.15: 'No, nor has Herod, for he sent Him back to us; and behold, nothing deserving death has been done by Him.
NASB.16: 'Therefore I will punish Him and release Him.'
NASB.17: [Now he was obliged to release to them at the feast one prisoner].
NASB.18: But they cried out all together, saying, 'Away with this man, and release for us Barabbas!'
NASB.19: (He was one who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection made in the city, and for murder.)
NASB.20: Pilate, wanting to release Jesus, addressed them again,
NASB.21: but they kept on calling out, saying, 'Crucify, crucify Him!'
NASB.22: And he said to them the third time, 'Why, what evil has this man done? I have found in Him no guilt [demanding] death; therefore I will punish Him and release Him.'
NASB.23: But they were insistent, with loud voices asking that He be crucified. And their voices [began] to prevail.
NASB.24: And Pilate pronounced sentence that their demand be granted.
NASB.25: And he released the man they were asking for who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, but he delivered Jesus to their will.
NASB.26: When they led Him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, coming in from the country, and placed on him the cross to carry behind Jesus.
NASB.27: And following Him was a large crowd of the people, and of women who were mourning and lamenting Him.
NASB.28: But Jesus turning to them said, 'Daughters of Jerusalem, stop weeping for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
NASB.29: 'For behold, the days are coming when they will say, 'Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.'
NASB.30: 'Then they will begin TO SAY TO THE MOUNTAINS, 'FALL ON US,' AND TO THE HILLS, 'COVER US.'
NASB.31: 'For if they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?'
NASB.32: Two others also, who were criminals, were being led away to be put to death with Him.
NASB.33: When they came to the place called The Skull, there they crucified Him and the criminals, one on the right and the other on the left.
NASB.34: But Jesus was saying, 'Father, forgive them; for they do not know what they are doing.' And they cast lots, dividing up His garments among themselves.
NASB.35: And the people stood by, looking on. And even the rulers were sneering at Him, saying, 'He saved others; let Him save Himself if this is the Christ of God, His Chosen One.'
NASB.36: The soldiers also mocked Him, coming up to Him, offering Him sour wine,
NASB.37: and saying, 'If You are the King of the Jews, save Yourself!'
NASB.38: Now there was also an inscription above Him, 'THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.'
NASB.39: One of the criminals who were hanged [there] was hurling abuse at Him, saying, 'Are You not the Christ? Save Yourself and us!'
NASB.40: But the other answered, and rebuking him said, 'Do you not even fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
NASB.41: 'And we indeed [are suffering] justly, for we are receiving what we deserve for our deeds; but this man has done nothing wrong.'
NASB.42: And he was saying, 'Jesus, remember me when You come in Your kingdom!'
NASB.43: And He said to him, 'Truly I say to you, today you shall be with Me in Paradise.'
NASB.44: It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness fell over the whole land until the ninth hour,
NASB.45: because the sun was obscured; and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
NASB.46: And Jesus, crying out with a loud voice, said, 'Father, INTO YOUR HANDS I COMMIT MY SPIRIT.' Having said this, He breathed His last.
NASB.47: Now when the centurion saw what had happened, he [began] praising God, saying, 'Certainly this man was innocent.'
NASB.48: And all the crowds who came together for this spectacle, when they observed what had happened, [began] to return, beating their breasts.
NASB.49: And all His acquaintances and the women who accompanied Him from Galilee were standing at a distance, seeing these things.
NASB.50: And a man named Joseph, who was a member of the Council, a good and righteous man
NASB.51: (he had not consented to their plan and action), [a man] from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who was waiting for the kingdom of God;
NASB.52: this man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
NASB.53: And he took it down and wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid Him in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had ever lain.
NASB.54: It was the preparation day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
NASB.55: Now the women who had come with Him out of Galilee followed, and saw the tomb and how His body was laid.
NASB.56: Then they returned and prepared spices and perfumes. And on the Sabbath they rested according to the commandment.

NLT.1: Then the entire council took Jesus to Pilate, the Roman governor.
NLT.2: They began to state their case: “This man has been leading our people astray by telling them not to pay their taxes to the Roman government and by claiming he is the Messiah, a king.”
NLT.3: So Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” Jesus replied, “You have said it.”
NLT.4: Pilate turned to the leading priests and to the crowd and said, “I find nothing wrong with this man!”
NLT.5: Then they became insistent. “But he is causing riots by his teaching wherever he goes — all over Judea, from Galilee to Jerusalem!”
NLT.6: “Oh, is he a Galilean?” Pilate asked.
NLT.7: When they said that he was, Pilate sent him to Herod Antipas, because Galilee was under Herod’s jurisdiction, and Herod happened to be in Jerusalem at the time.
NLT.8: Herod was delighted at the opportunity to see Jesus, because he had heard about him and had been hoping for a long time to see him perform a miracle.
NLT.9: He asked Jesus question after question, but Jesus refused to answer.
NLT.10: Meanwhile, the leading priests and the teachers of religious law stood there shouting their accusations.
NLT.11: Then Herod and his soldiers began mocking and ridiculing Jesus. Finally, they put a royal robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
NLT.12: (Herod and Pilate, who had been enemies before, became friends that day.)
NLT.13: Then Pilate called together the leading priests and other religious leaders, along with the people,
NLT.14: and he announced his verdict. “You brought this man to me, accusing him of leading a revolt. I have examined him thoroughly on this point in your presence and find him innocent.
NLT.15: Herod came to the same conclusion and sent him back to us. Nothing this man has done calls for the death penalty.
NLT.16: So I will have him flogged, and then I will release him.”
NLT.18: Then a mighty roar rose from the crowd, and with one voice they shouted, “Kill him, and release Barabbas to us!”
NLT.19: (Barabbas was in prison for taking part in an insurrection in Jerusalem against the government, and for murder.)
NLT.20: Pilate argued with them, because he wanted to release Jesus.
NLT.21: But they kept shouting, “Crucify him! Crucify him!”
NLT.22: For the third time he demanded, “Why? What crime has he committed? I have found no reason to sentence him to death. So I will have him flogged, and then I will release him.”
NLT.23: But the mob shouted louder and louder, demanding that Jesus be crucified, and their voices prevailed.
NLT.24: So Pilate sentenced Jesus to die as they demanded.
NLT.25: As they had requested, he released Barabbas, the man in prison for insurrection and murder. But he turned Jesus over to them to do as they wished.
NLT.26: As they led Jesus away, a man named Simon, who was from Cyrene, happened to be coming in from the countryside. The soldiers seized him and put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
NLT.27: A large crowd trailed behind, including many grief-stricken women.
NLT.28: But Jesus turned and said to them, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
NLT.29: For the days are coming when they will say, ‘Fortunate indeed are the women who are childless, the wombs that have not borne a child and the breasts that have never nursed.’
NLT.30: People will beg the mountains, ‘Fall on us,’ and plead with the hills, ‘Bury us.’
NLT.31: For if these things are done when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry? ”
NLT.32: Two others, both criminals, were led out to be executed with him.
NLT.33: When they came to a place called The Skull, they nailed him to the cross. And the criminals were also crucified — one on his right and one on his left.
NLT.34: Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” And the soldiers gambled for his clothes by throwing dice.
NLT.35: The crowd watched and the leaders scoffed. “He saved others,” they said, “let him save himself if he is really God’s Messiah, the Chosen One.”
NLT.36: The soldiers mocked him, too, by offering him a drink of sour wine.
NLT.37: They called out to him, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
NLT.38: A sign was fastened above him with these words: “This is the King of the Jews.”
NLT.39: One of the criminals hanging beside him scoffed, “So you’re the Messiah, are you? Prove it by saving yourself — and us, too, while you’re at it!”
NLT.40: But the other criminal protested, “Don’t you fear God even when you have been sentenced to die?
NLT.41: We deserve to die for our crimes, but this man hasn’t done anything wrong.”
NLT.42: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”
NLT.43: And Jesus replied, “I assure you, today you will be with me in paradise.”
NLT.44: By this time it was about noon, and darkness fell across the whole land until three o’clock.
NLT.45: The light from the sun was gone. And suddenly, the curtain in the sanctuary of the Temple was torn down the middle.
NLT.46: Then Jesus shouted, “Father, I entrust my spirit into your hands!” And with those words he breathed his last.
NLT.47: When the Roman officer overseeing the execution saw what had happened, he worshiped God and said, “Surely this man was innocent. ”
NLT.48: And when all the crowd that came to see the crucifixion saw what had happened, they went home in deep sorrow.
NLT.49: But Jesus’ friends, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance watching.
NLT.50: Now there was a good and righteous man named Joseph. He was a member of the Jewish high council,
NLT.51: but he had not agreed with the decision and actions of the other religious leaders. He was from the town of Arimathea in Judea, and he was waiting for the Kingdom of God to come.
NLT.52: He went to Pilate and asked for Jesus’ body.
NLT.53: Then he took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in a long sheet of linen cloth and laid it in a new tomb that had been carved out of rock.
NLT.54: This was done late on Friday afternoon, the day of preparation, as the Sabbath was about to begin.
NLT.55: As his body was taken away, the women from Galilee followed and saw the tomb where his body was placed.
NLT.56: Then they went home and prepared spices and ointments to anoint his body. But by the time they were finished the Sabbath had begun, so they rested as required by the law.

WEB.1: The whole company of them rose up and brought him before Pilate.
WEB.2: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting the nation, forbidding paying taxes to Caesar, and saying that he himself is Christ, a king.”
WEB.3: Pilate asked him, “Are you the King of the Jews?” He answered him, “So you say.”
WEB.4: Pilate said to the chief priests and the multitudes, “I find no basis for a charge against this man.”
WEB.5: But they insisted, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee even to this place.”
WEB.6: But when Pilate heard Galilee mentioned, he asked if the man was a Galilean.
WEB.7: When he found out that he was in Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem during those days.
WEB.8: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him. He hoped to see some miracle done by him.
WEB.9: He questioned him with many words, but he gave no answers.
WEB.10: The chief priests and the scribes stood, vehemently accusing him.
WEB.11: Herod with his soldiers humiliated him and mocked him. Dressing him in luxurious clothing, they sent him back to Pilate.
WEB.12: Herod and Pilate became friends with each other that very day, for before that they were enemies with each other.
WEB.13: Pilate called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
WEB.14: and said to them, “You brought this man to me as one that perverts the people, and see, I have examined him before you, and found no basis for a charge against this man concerning those things of which you accuse him.
WEB.15: Neither has Herod, for I sent you to him, and see, nothing worthy of death has been done by him.
WEB.16: I will therefore chastise him and release him.”
WEB.17: Now he had to release one prisoner to them at the feast.
WEB.18: But they all cried out together, saying, “Away with this man! Release to us Barabbas!” —
WEB.19: one who was thrown into prison for a certain revolt in the city, and for murder.
WEB.20: Then Pilate spoke to them again, wanting to release Jesus,
WEB.21: but they shouted, saying, “Crucify! Crucify him!”
WEB.22: He said to them the third time, “Why? What evil has this man done? I have found no capital crime in him. I will therefore chastise him and release him.”
WEB.23: But they were urgent with loud voices, asking that he might be crucified. Their voices and the voices of the chief priests prevailed.
WEB.24: Pilate decreed that what they asked for should be done.
WEB.25: He released him who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, for whom they asked, but he delivered Jesus up to their will.
WEB.26: When they led him away, they grabbed one Simon of Cyrene, coming from the country, and laid on him the cross, to carry it after Jesus.
WEB.27: A great multitude of the people followed him, including women who also mourned and lamented him.
WEB.28: But Jesus, turning to them, said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, don’t weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
WEB.29: For behold, the days are coming in which they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’
WEB.30: Then they will begin to tell the mountains, ‘Fall on us!’ and tell the hills, ‘Cover us.’
WEB.31: For if they do these things in the green tree, what will be done in the dry?”

WEB.42: He said to Jesus, “Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”

WEB.45: The sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was torn in two.
WEB.46: Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit!” Having said this, he breathed his last.

WEB.51: (he had not consented to their counsel and deed), from Arimathaea, a city of the Jews, who was also waiting for God’s Kingdom:

CSB17.1: Then <sup>ⓗ</sup> their whole assembly rose up and brought him before Pilate.

CSB17.3: So Pilate asked him, <sup>ⓙ</sup> “Are you the king of the Jews?” <sup>ⓚ</sup> He answered him, “You say so.” <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.4: Pilate then told the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no grounds <sup>ⓛ</sup> for charging this man.”
CSB17.5: But they kept insisting, “He stirs up the people, <sup>ⓜ</sup> teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee <sup>ⓝ</sup> where he started even to here.”
CSB17.6: When Pilate <sup>ⓐ</sup> heard this, <sup>[2]</sup> he asked if the man was a Galilean.
CSB17.7: Finding that he was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, <sup>ⓑ</sup> who was also in Jerusalem during those days.
CSB17.8: Herod was very glad to see Jesus; for a long time he had wanted to see him because he had heard about him and was hoping to see some miracle <sup>[3]</sup> performed by him.
CSB17.9: So he kept asking him questions, but Jesus did not answer him. <sup>ⓒ</sup>

CSB17.13: Pilate called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.14: and said to them, “You have brought me this man as one who misleads the people. <sup>ⓗ</sup> But in fact, after examining him in your presence, I have found no grounds <sup>ⓘ</sup> to charge this man with those things you accuse him of.
CSB17.15: Neither has Herod, because he sent him back to us. Clearly, he has done nothing to deserve death.

CSB17.19: (He had been thrown into prison for a rebellion that had taken place in the city, and for murder.)
CSB17.20: Wanting to release Jesus, <sup>ⓜ</sup> Pilate addressed them again,
CSB17.21: but they kept shouting, “Crucify! Crucify him!”
CSB17.22: A third time he said to them, “Why? What has this man done wrong? <sup>ⓝ</sup> I have found in him no grounds <sup>ⓐ</sup> for the death penalty. Therefore, I will have him whipped and then release him.”
CSB17.23: But they kept up the pressure, demanding with loud voices that he be crucified, and their voices <sup>[7]</sup> won out.
CSB17.24: So <sup>ⓑ</sup> Pilate decided to grant their demand <sup>ⓒ</sup>

CSB17.33: When <sup>ⓐ</sup> they arrived at the place called The Skull, they crucified him there, along with the criminals, one on the right and one on the left.
CSB17.34: Then Jesus said, “Father, <sup>ⓑ</sup>forgive <sup>ⓒ</sup>them, because they do not know what they are doing.” <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup> And they divided his clothes and cast lots. <sup>ⓔ</sup>

CSB17.36: The soldiers also mocked him. They came offering him sour wine <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.37: and said, “If you are the King of the Jews, <sup>ⓙ</sup> save yourself!”
CSB17.38: An inscription was above him: <sup>[10]</sup> THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
CSB17.39: Then <sup>ⓚ</sup> one of the criminals hanging there began to yell insults at <sup>[11]</sup> him: “Aren’t you the Messiah? Save yourself and us!”

CSB17.42: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me <sup>[12]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> when you come into your kingdom.” <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.43: And he said to him, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.44: It <sup>ⓒ</sup> was now about noon, <sup>[13]</sup> and darkness came over the whole land <sup>[14]</sup> until three, <sup>[15]</sup>
CSB17.45: because the sun’s light failed. <sup>[16]</sup> The curtain <sup>ⓓ</sup> of the sanctuary was split down the middle.
CSB17.46: And Jesus called out with a loud voice, “Father, into your hands I entrust my spirit.” [17] ⓔ Saying this, he breathed his last.
CSB17.47: When the centurion saw what happened, he began to glorify <sup>ⓕ</sup> God, saying, “This man really was righteous!” <sup>[18]</sup>
CSB17.48: All the crowds that had gathered for this spectacle, when they saw what had taken place, went home, striking their chests. <sup>ⓖ</sup>

AMP.1: Then the whole assembly got up and brought Him before Pilate.
AMP.2: They began to accuse Jesus, asserting, "We found this Man misleading and perverting our nation and forbidding us to pay taxes to Caesar, and claiming that He Himself is Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed), a King."
AMP.3: So Pilate asked Him, "Are You the King of the Jews?" And He answered him, "It is just as you say."
AMP.4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, "I find no guilt in this Man."
AMP.5: But they were insistent and said, "He stirs up the people [to rebel], teaching throughout Judea, starting from Galilee even as far as here [in Jerusalem]."
AMP.6: When Pilate heard it, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
AMP.7: And when he learned that He belonged to the jurisdiction of Herod [Antipas, the tetrarch of Galilee], he sent Him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
AMP.8: When Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly pleased. He had wanted to see Him for a long time because of what he had heard about Him, and was hoping to see some [miraculous] sign [even something spectacular] done by Him.
AMP.9: And he questioned Him at some length, but Jesus made no reply.
AMP.10: The chief priests and the scribes were standing there, continually accusing Him heatedly.
AMP.11: And Herod with his soldiers, after treating Him with contempt and mocking and ridiculing Him, dressed Him in a gorgeous robe and sent Him back to Pilate.
AMP.12: Now that very day Herod and Pilate became friends with each other—before this they had been enemies.
AMP.13: Pilate summoned the chief priests and the rulers and the people,
AMP.14: and said to them, "You brought this man before me as one who corrupts and incites the people to rebellion. After examining Him before you, I have found no guilt in this Man regarding the charges which you make against Him.
AMP.15: No, nor has Herod, for he sent Him back to us; and indeed, He has done nothing to deserve death.
AMP.16: Therefore I will punish Him [to teach Him a lesson] and release Him."
AMP.17: [Now he was obligated to release to them one prisoner at the Feast.]
AMP.18: But they [loudly] shouted out all together, saying, "Away with this Man, and release Barabbas to us!"
AMP.19: (He was one who had been thrown into prison for an insurrection that happened in the city, and for murder.)
AMP.20: Pilate addressed them again, wanting to release Jesus,
AMP.21: but they kept shouting out, "Crucify, crucify Him!"
AMP.22: A third time he said to them, "Why, what wrong has He done? I have found no guilt [no crime, no offense] in Him demanding death; therefore I will punish Him [to teach Him a lesson] and release Him."
AMP.23: But they were insistent and unrelenting, demanding with loud voices that Jesus be crucified. And their voices began to prevail and accomplish their purpose.
AMP.24: Pilate pronounced sentence that their demand be granted.
AMP.25: And he released the man they were asking for who had been thrown into prison for insurrection and murder, but he handed over Jesus to their will.
AMP.26: When they led Him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming in [to the city] from the country, and placed on him the cross to carry behind Jesus.
AMP.27: Following Him was a large crowd of the people, including women who were mourning and wailing for Him.
AMP.28: But Jesus, turning toward them, said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for Me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
AMP.29: For behold, the days are coming when they will say, 'Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that have not given birth, and the breasts that have never nursed.'
AMP.30: Then they will begin <e>to say to the mountains, 'Fall on us!' and to the hills, 'Cover us</e>!'
AMP.31: For if they do these things when the tree is green, what will happen when it is dry?"
AMP.32: Two others also, who were criminals, were being led away to be executed with Him.
AMP.33: When they came to the place called The Skull, there they crucified Him and the criminals, one on the right and one on the left.
AMP.34: And Jesus was saying, "Father, forgive them; for they do not know what they are doing." And they cast lots, dividing His clothes among themselves.
AMP.35: Now the people stood by, watching; but even the rulers ridiculed and sneered at Him, saying, "He saved others [from death]; let Him save Himself if He is the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed) of God, His Chosen One."
AMP.36: The soldiers also mocked Him, coming up to Him and [cruelly] offering Him sour wine,
AMP.37: and sarcastically saying, "If you are [really] the King of the Jews, save Yourself [from death]!"
AMP.38: Now there was also an inscription above Him: "THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS."
AMP.39: One of the criminals who had been hanged [on a cross beside Him] kept hurling abuse at Him, saying, "Are You not the Christ? Save Yourself and us [from death]!"
AMP.40: But the other one rebuked him, saying, "Do you not even fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
AMP.41: We are suffering justly, because we are getting what we deserve for what we have done; but this Man has done nothing wrong."
AMP.42: And he was saying, "Jesus, [please] remember me when You come into Your kingdom!"
AMP.43: Jesus said to him, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, today you will be with Me in Paradise."
AMP.44: It was now about the sixth hour (noon), and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour (3:00 p.m.),
AMP.45: because the sun was obscured; and the veil [of the Holy of Holies] of the temple was torn in two [from top to bottom].
AMP.46: And Jesus, crying out with a loud voice, said, "Father, <e>into Your hands I commit My spirit</e>!" Having said this, He breathed His last.
AMP.47: Now when the centurion saw what had taken place, he began praising and honoring God, saying, "Certainly this Man was innocent."
AMP.48: All the crowds who had gathered for this spectacle, when they saw what had happened, began to return [to their homes], beating their breasts [as a sign of mourning or repentance].
AMP.49: And all His acquaintances and the women who had accompanied Him from Galilee were standing at a distance, watching these things.
AMP.50: A man named Joseph, who was a member of the Council (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court), a good and honorable man
AMP.51: (he had not consented to the Council's plan and action) a man from Arimathea, a city of the Jews, who was waiting for and expecting the kingdom of God;
AMP.52: this man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
AMP.53: And [after receiving permission] he took it down and wrapped it in a linen [burial] cloth and laid Him in a tomb cut into the rock, where no one had yet been laid.
AMP.54: It was the day of preparation [for the Sabbath], and the Sabbath was dawning.
AMP.55: Now the women who had come with Him from Galilee followed [closely], and saw the tomb and how His body was laid.
AMP.56:
Then they went back and prepared spices and ointments and sweet-smelling herbs.
And on the Sabbath they rested in accordance with the commandment [forbidding work].

MSG.1: Then they all took Jesus to Pilate
MSG.2: and began to bring up charges against him. They said, "We found this man undermining our law and order, forbidding taxes to be paid to Caesar, setting himself up as Messiah-King."
MSG.3: Pilate asked him, "Is this true that you're 'King of the Jews'?" "Those are your words, not mine," Jesus replied.
MSG.4: Pilate told the high priests and the accompanying crowd, "I find nothing wrong here. He seems harmless enough to me."
MSG.5: But they were vehement. "He's stirring up unrest among the people with his teaching, disturbing the peace everywhere, starting in Galilee and now all through Judea. He's a dangerous man, endangering the peace."
MSG.6: When Pilate heard that, he asked, "So, he's a Galilean?"
MSG.7: Realizing that he properly came under Herod's jurisdiction, he passed the buck to Herod, who just happened to be in Jerusalem for a few days.
MSG.8: Herod was delighted when Jesus showed up. He had wanted for a long time to see him, he'd heard so much about him. He hoped to see him do something spectacular.
MSG.9: He peppered him with questions. Jesus didn't answer—not one word.
MSG.10: But the high priests and religion scholars were right there, saying their piece, strident and shrill in their accusations.
MSG.11: Mightily offended, Herod turned on Jesus. His soldiers joined in, taunting and jeering. Then they dressed him up in an elaborate king costume and sent him back to Pilate.
MSG.12: That day Herod and Pilate became thick as thieves. Always before they had kept their distance.
MSG.13: Then Pilate called in the high priests, rulers, and the others
MSG.14: and said, "You brought this man to me as a disturber of the peace. I examined him in front of all of you and found there was nothing to your charge.
MSG.15: And neither did Herod, for he has sent him back here with a clean bill of health. It's clear that he's done nothing wrong, let alone anything deserving death.
MSG.16: I'm going to warn him to watch his step and let him go."
MSG.18: At that, the crowd went wild: "Kill him! Give us Barabbas!"
MSG.19: (Barabbas had been thrown in prison for starting a riot in the city and for murder.)
MSG.20: Pilate still wanted to let Jesus go, and so spoke out again.
MSG.21: But they kept shouting back, "Crucify! Crucify him!"
MSG.22: He tried a third time. "But for what crime? I've found nothing in him deserving death. I'm going to warn him to watch his step and let him go."
MSG.23: But they kept at it, a shouting mob, demanding that he be crucified. And finally they shouted him down.
MSG.24: Pilate caved in and gave them what they wanted.
MSG.25: He released the man thrown in prison for rioting and murder, and gave them Jesus to do whatever they wanted.
MSG.26: As they led him off, they made Simon, a man from Cyrene who happened to be coming in from the countryside, carry the cross behind Jesus.
MSG.27: A huge crowd of people followed, along with women weeping and carrying on.
MSG.28: At one point Jesus turned to the women and said, "Daughters of Jerusalem, don't cry for me. Cry for yourselves and for your children.
MSG.29: The time is coming when they'll say, 'Lucky the women who never conceived! Lucky the wombs that never gave birth! Lucky the breasts that never gave milk!'
MSG.30: Then they'll start calling to the mountains, 'Fall down on us!' calling to the hills, 'Cover us up!'
MSG.31: If people do these things to a live, green tree, can you imagine what they'll do with deadwood?"
MSG.32: Two others, both criminals, were taken along with him for execution.
MSG.33: When they got to the place called Skull Hill, they crucified him, along with the criminals, one on his right, the other on his left.
MSG.34: Jesus prayed, "Father, forgive them; they don't know what they're doing." Dividing up his clothes, they threw dice for them.
MSG.35: The people stood there staring at Jesus, and the ringleaders made faces, taunting, "He saved others. Let's see him save himself! The Messiah of God—ha! The Chosen—ha!"
MSG.36: The soldiers also came up and poked fun at him, making a game of it. They toasted him with sour wine:
MSG.37: "So you're King of the Jews! Save yourself!"
MSG.38: Printed over him was a sign: this is the king of the jews.
MSG.39: One of the criminals hanging alongside cursed him: "Some Messiah you are! Save yourself! Save us!"
MSG.40: But the other one made him shut up: "Have you no fear of God? You're getting the same as him.
MSG.41: We deserve this, but not him—he did nothing to deserve this."
MSG.42: Then he said, "Jesus, remember me when you enter your kingdom."
MSG.43: He said, "Don't worry, I will. Today you will join me in paradise."
MSG.44: By now it was noon. The whole earth became dark, the darkness lasting three hours—
MSG.45: a total blackout. The Temple curtain split right down the middle.
MSG.46: Jesus called loudly, "Father, I place my life in your hands!" Then he breathed his last.
MSG.47: When the captain there saw what happened, he honored God: "This man was innocent! A good man, and innocent!"
MSG.48: All who had come around as spectators to watch the show, when they saw what actually happened, were overcome with grief and headed home.
MSG.49: Those who knew Jesus well, along with the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a respectful distance and kept vigil.
MSG.50: There was a man by the name of Joseph, a member of the Jewish High Council, a man of good heart and good character.
MSG.51: He had not gone along with the plans and actions of the council. His hometown was the Jewish village of Arimathea.
MSG.52: He lived in alert expectation of the kingdom of God. He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
MSG.53: Taking him down, he wrapped him in a linen shroud and placed him in a tomb chiseled into the rock, a tomb never yet used.
MSG.54: It was the day before Sabbath, the Sabbath just about to begin.
MSG.55: The women who had been companions of Jesus from Galilee followed along. They saw the tomb where Jesus' body was placed.
MSG.56: Then they went back to prepare burial spices and perfumes. They rested quietly on the Sabbath, as commanded.

NIV.43: Jesus answered him, “I tell you the truth, today you will be with me in paradise.”
NIV.44:
Jesus’ Death
It was now about the sixth hour, and darkness came over the whole land until the ninth hour,
NIV.45: for the sun stopped shining. And the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
NIV.46: Jesus called out with a loud voice, “Father, into your hands I commit my spirit.” When he had said this, he breathed his last.
NIV.47: The centurion, seeing what had happened, praised God and said, “Surely this was a righteous man.”
NIV.48: When all the people who had gathered to witness this sight saw what took place, they beat their breasts and went away.
NIV.49: But all those who knew him, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
NIV.50:
Jesus’ Burial
Now there was a man named Joseph, a member of the Council, a good and upright man,
NIV.51: who had not consented to their decision and action. He came from the Judean town of Arimathea and he was waiting for the kingdom of God.
NIV.52: Going to Pilate, he asked for Jesus’ body.
NIV.53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in linen cloth and placed it in a tomb cut in the rock, one in which no one had yet been laid.
NIV.54: It was Preparation Day, and the Sabbath was about to begin.
NIV.55: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and saw the tomb and how his body was laid in it.
NIV.56: Then they went home and prepared spices and perfumes. But they rested on the Sabbath in obedience to the commandment.

NRSVCE.1: Then the assembly rose as a body and brought Jesus before Pilate.
NRSVCE.2: They began to accuse him, saying, “We found this man perverting our nation, forbidding us to pay taxes to the emperor, and saying that he himself is the Messiah, a king.”
NRSVCE.3: Then Pilate asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” He answered, “You say so.”
NRSVCE.4: Then Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowds, “I find no basis for an accusation against this man.”
NRSVCE.5: But they were insistent and said, “He stirs up the people by teaching throughout all Judea, from Galilee where he began even to this place.”
NRSVCE.6: When Pilate heard this, he asked whether the man was a Galilean.
NRSVCE.7: And when he learned that he was under Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him off to Herod, who was himself in Jerusalem at that time.
NRSVCE.8: When Herod saw Jesus, he was very glad, for he had been wanting to see him for a long time, because he had heard about him and was hoping to see him perform some sign.
NRSVCE.9: He questioned him at some length, but Jesus gave him no answer.
NRSVCE.10: The chief priests and the scribes stood by, vehemently accusing him.
NRSVCE.11: Even Herod with his soldiers treated him with contempt and mocked him; then he put an elegant robe on him, and sent him back to Pilate.
NRSVCE.12: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends with each other; before this they had been enemies.
NRSVCE.13: Pilate then called together the chief priests, the leaders, and the people,
NRSVCE.14: and said to them, “You brought me this man as one who was perverting the people; and here I have examined him in your presence and have not found this man guilty of any of your charges against him.
NRSVCE.15: Neither has Herod, for he sent him back to us. Indeed, he has done nothing to deserve death.
NRSVCE.16: I will therefore have him flogged and release him.”
NRSVCE.18: Then they all shouted out together, “Away with this fellow! Release Barabbas for us!”
NRSVCE.19: (This was a man who had been put in prison for an insurrection that had taken place in the city, and for murder.)
NRSVCE.20: Pilate, wanting to release Jesus, addressed them again;
NRSVCE.21: but they kept shouting, “Crucify, crucify him!”
NRSVCE.22: A third time he said to them, “Why, what evil has he done? I have found in him no ground for the sentence of death; I will therefore have him flogged and then release him.”
NRSVCE.23: But they kept urgently demanding with loud shouts that he should be crucified; and their voices prevailed.
NRSVCE.24: So Pilate gave his verdict that their demand should be granted.
NRSVCE.25: He released the man they asked for, the one who had been put in prison for insurrection and murder, and he handed Jesus over as they wished.
NRSVCE.26: As they led him away, they seized a man, Simon of Cyrene, who was coming from the country, and they laid the cross on him, and made him carry it behind Jesus.
NRSVCE.27: A great number of the people followed him, and among them were women who were beating their breasts and wailing for him.
NRSVCE.28: But Jesus turned to them and said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
NRSVCE.29: For the days are surely coming when they will say, ‘Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never bore, and the breasts that never nursed.’
NRSVCE.30: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, ‘Fall on us’; and to the hills, ‘Cover us.’
NRSVCE.31: For if they do this when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?”
NRSVCE.32: Two others also, who were criminals, were led away to be put to death with him.
NRSVCE.33: When they came to the place that is called The Skull, they crucified Jesus there with the criminals, one on his right and one on his left.
NRSVCE.34: [[Then Jesus said, “Father, forgive them; for they do not know what they are doing.”]] And they cast lots to divide his clothing.
NRSVCE.35: And the people stood by, watching; but the leaders scoffed at him, saying, “He saved others; let him save himself if he is the Messiah of God, his chosen one!”
NRSVCE.36: The soldiers also mocked him, coming up and offering him sour wine,
NRSVCE.37: and saying, “If you are the King of the Jews, save yourself!”
NRSVCE.38: There was also an inscription over him, “This is the King of the Jews.”
NRSVCE.39: One of the criminals who were hanged there kept deriding him and saying, “Are you not the Messiah? Save yourself and us!”
NRSVCE.40: But the other rebuked him, saying, “Do you not fear God, since you are under the same sentence of condemnation?
NRSVCE.41: And we indeed have been condemned justly, for we are getting what we deserve for our deeds, but this man has done nothing wrong.”
NRSVCE.42: Then he said, “Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.”
NRSVCE.43: He replied, “Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise.”
NRSVCE.44: It was now about noon, and darkness came over the whole land until three in the afternoon,
NRSVCE.45: while the sun’s light failed; and the curtain of the temple was torn in two.
NRSVCE.46: Then Jesus, crying with a loud voice, said, “Father, into your hands I commend my spirit.” Having said this, he breathed his last.
NRSVCE.47: When the centurion saw what had taken place, he praised God and said, “Certainly this man was innocent.”
NRSVCE.48: And when all the crowds who had gathered there for this spectacle saw what had taken place, they returned home, beating their breasts.
NRSVCE.49: But all his acquaintances, including the women who had followed him from Galilee, stood at a distance, watching these things.
NRSVCE.50: Now there was a good and righteous man named Joseph, who, though a member of the council,
NRSVCE.51: had not agreed to their plan and action. He came from the Jewish town of Arimathea, and he was waiting expectantly for the kingdom of God.
NRSVCE.52: This man went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
NRSVCE.53: Then he took it down, wrapped it in a linen cloth, and laid it in a rock-hewn tomb where no one had ever been laid.
NRSVCE.54: It was the day of Preparation, and the sabbath was beginning.
NRSVCE.55: The women who had come with him from Galilee followed, and they saw the tomb and how his body was laid.
NRSVCE.56: Then they returned, and prepared spices and ointments. On the sabbath they rested according to the commandment.
